economic_notes: First Nation is a beneficial owner of 12.5% of Shawano Wapunong Investments Corp. which invests in commercial real estate in Winnipeg. First Nation has a legal claim against the Government of Canada financed by a demand loan up to $4,669,000. Consolidated subsidiaries include Black River Development Corporation and Black River Land Development Ltd.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to inability to determine completeness of Asset Retirement Obligation (ARO) liabilities under PS 3280. Emphasis of Matter regarding a demand loan payable up to $4,669,000 used to pursue a legal claim against the Government of Canada — no corresponding asset recorded. Prior year figures restated due to correction of an error (Note 22) reducing opening accumulated surplus by $1,742,375. First Nation is not in compliance with CMHC replacement reserve and operating surplus reserve requirements. Construction in progress of $3,471,394 is reported separately from tangible capital assets. expenses_housing combines Housing ($518,133) and Subsidized Housing ($756,185). expenses_salaries_total taken from cash paid to employees in cash flow statement. total_government_transfers includes ISC ($17,547,147), CMHC ($370,394), and Assembly of Manitoba Chiefs ($388,440) = $18,305,981. OSR = $20,541,881 - $18,305,981 = $2,235,900. Economic Development expenses: $446,105; Fundraising expenses: $1,240,585 not mapped to standard categories.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
High ISC dependency (85.42%) — operations heavily reliant on federal transfers
